key_insight: Geoalign stabilizes online LLM reinforcement learning by detecting and replacing reward-driven rollouts whose representation-space update directions conflict with the batch consensus.
engineering_relevance: As a lightweight forward-pass curation plug-in, Geoalign can improve reliability in reward-based LLM training without adding meaningful runtime overhead.
method: Forms within-prompt preference pairs from rollouts.
Learns an online projector on hidden states to align reward-ordered displacement directions.
Flags angular outliers against a batch consensus prototype and swaps them with stable alternatives.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improvements and reduced training oscillation on both dialogue alignment with a learned reward model and mathematical reasoning with binary verified rewards, outperforming PF-PPO, PAR, PODS, and Seed-GRPO.
watch_item: Because Geoalign depends on batch-level angular consensus and within-prompt alternatives, its effectiveness may be sensitive to reward noise, batch composition, and the availability of stable replacement rollouts.
paper_title: GEOALIGN: Geometric Rollout Curation for Robust LLM Reinforcement Learning
